---
title: "Home"
image: images/2024_headshot_primary.jpg   # put the headshot *here*
about:
  template: trestles                      # required (e.g., jolla | trestles | solana | marquee | broadside)
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/jasmin-baier
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/jasminbaier/
---

I am a doctoral candidate in Behavioral Economics and Public Policy at the Blavatnik School of Government, University of Oxford. My research interests lie at the intersection of behavioral and development economics, with a particular focus on (soft) skills, AI and labor markets, and how humans integrate other people's preferences in their decision-making.

Prior to commencing my doctoral studies, I gained extensive experience in applied research through my role at the Busara Center for Behavioral Economics in Nairobi, where I led and managed field-based projects on cash transfers and educational migration. I also contributed to private sector development initiatives with the World Bank Group in Uganda and held a data science position at the World Data Lab, where I worked on global development metrics.

My early professional experiences at the European Commission and the Austrian National Bank cultivated a longstanding interest in evidence-based policymaking. I hold a B.Sc. in Socio-Economics from WU Vienna and an M.A. in Development Economics from Yale University, where I studied as a Fulbright Scholar.

_favicon AI by Palash Jain from <a href="https://thenounproject.com/browse/icons/term/ai/" target="_blank" title="AI Icons">Noun Project</a> (CC BY 3.0)_
